title: Autoclave Biohazard Waste Protocol
author: Steven W Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6cyhaxw
materials: Autoclave the processed waste on gravity cycle.

steps.title: If the waste container is >...
steps.instruction: If the waste container is > ¾ full, then it needs to be processed for autoclaving.

steps.title: Instructions on cycle times based on...
steps.instruction: Instructions on cycle times based on the amount of trash you are autoclaving are on the autoclave.

steps.title: Transfer bag into a wide autoclavable tray
steps.instruction: Transfer bag into a wide autoclavable tray. Tray will have PP on bottom. Don't use HDPE! It will MELT !

steps.title: Pour 100 mL di water into bag containing trash
steps.instruction: Pour 100 mL di water into bag containing trash.

steps.title: Pour 100 mL di water into tray
steps.instruction: Pour 100 mL di water into tray. Between the container and the bag.

steps.title: Close bag loosely put a strip...
steps.instruction: Close bag loosely put a strip of autoclave tape on bag.

steps.title: After autoclaved waste has cooled, put...
steps.instruction: After autoclaved waste has cooled, put the autoclaved waste into designated trash bin next to autoclave.

steps.title: Transfer sharps container into a wide autoclavable tray
steps.instruction: Transfer sharps container into a wide autoclavable tray. Tray will have PP on bottom. Don't use HDPE! It will MELT!

steps.title: Pour 100 mL water into interior of the sharps container
steps.instruction: Pour 100 mL water into interior of the sharps container.

steps.title: Close lid until it snaps shut...
steps.instruction: Close lid until it snaps shut and put autoclave tape on lid.

steps.title: After autoclaved waste has cooled, put...
steps.instruction: After autoclaved waste has cooled, put the autoclaved waste into designated trash bin next to autoclave.
